Persist data-quality flags to the log backlog

Source: R/logs.R
eri_dq_log.Rd

Writes the $flags from a run_dq_checks() result (plus a corrections count) to a YAML log in {country}/{disease}/{data_source}[/{data_type}]/logs/ in the data/ Azure blob, so the data-quality issues are durable and discoverable by eri_logs(). Without this, run_dq_checks() flags exist only in your R session. eri_ingest() calls this automatically.

Usage

eri_dq_log(
  result,
  country,
  disease,
  data_source,
  data_type = NULL,
  period = NULL,
  data_con = NULL
)

Arguments

result

A dq_result object returned by run_dq_checks().

country

chr Country code (e.g. "uga").

disease

chr Disease name (e.g. "oncho").

data_source

chr The channel ("surveillance", "programmatic", "research").

data_type

chr or NULL The measure (e.g. "case", "treatment"); NULL (default) writes to the four-axis channel-level logs/ directory.

period

chr or NULL Reporting period the data covers (e.g. "2024-01").

data_con

Azure container for the data/ blob. If NULL, connects automatically.

Value

Invisibly, the number of flags logged.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
result <- run_dq_checks(raw, schema)
eri_dq_log(result, "uga", "oncho", "surveillance", period = "2024-01")
} # }
